title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Go Client Systems · Systems Transfer
summary: A backup automation team integrating an unfamiliar artifact service must satisfy changed endpoint, payload, authority, byte, and time constraints without a recipe for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Go 1.26 net/http typed response and failure classifier with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Status Families
steps.instruction:
lab case — Go backup artifact client observes 202 without a result, 204 with no content, 206 with a range, 429 with Retry-After, and 503 problem+json for case G-5. Preserve status, transport, schema, domain, partial, and unknown-effect outcomes separately. Competency probe: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Status Families, keep Status Families connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

content.title: Evidence defense
